meta.title: Zustand - Simple and Scalable State Management for React
meta.description: Learn Zustand, a small, fast and scalable state management solution for React. Complete documentation, tutorials, and examples.

hero.title: Zustand
hero.subtitle: A small, fast and scalable bearbones state-management solution
hero.description: Zustand is a simple and performant state management library for React applications. It has a minimal API, is easy to learn, and doesn't require boilerplate code.

simple.title: Simple & Minimal
simple.description: No providers, no context, just pure React hooks. Simple API that's easy to learn and use.

fast.title: Fast & Performant
fast.description: Optimized for performance with automatic render optimization and minimal re-renders.

scalable.title: Scalable
scalable.description: Works great for small apps and scales effortlessly to large, complex applications.

typescript.title: TypeScript First
typescript.description: Built with TypeScript in mind, providing excellent type inference and developer experience.

devtools.title: DevTools Support
devtools.description: Integrate with Redux DevTools for easy debugging and state inspection.

middleware.title: Flexible Middleware
middleware.description: Extend functionality with middleware for persistence, devtools, and more.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.step3: Use the store in your components

coreConcepts.title: Core Concepts
coreConcepts.storeDesc: A store holds your application state. It's created using the create function and returns a React hook.
coreConcepts.stateDesc: The state is the data that your store holds. It can be any JavaScript value - objects, arrays, primitives, etc.
coreConcepts.actionsDesc: Actions are functions that modify the state. They use the set function to update the store.
coreConcepts.selectorsDesc: Selectors allow you to subscribe to specific parts of the state, optimizing re-renders.

advanced.title: Advanced Topics
advanced.middlewareDesc: Extend Zustand with middleware for logging, persistence, and more.
advanced.persistDesc: Save your state to localStorage or sessionStorage automatically.
advanced.devtoolsDesc: Debug your state with Redux DevTools integration.
advanced.immerDesc: Use Immer for easier immutable state updates.

footer.description: Learn Zustand - A comprehensive guide to state management in React

create.title: create()
create.description: Creates a store with the given state creator function.
create.paramDesc: stateCreator - Function that receives set, get, and api
create.returnsDesc: A React hook that can be used to access the store

set.title: set()
set.description: Updates the store state. Can be called with a partial state object or an updater function.
set.paramPartial: partial - Partial state object or updater function
set.paramReplace: replace - (optional) Replace entire state instead of merging

get.title: get()
get.description: Gets the current state. Useful in actions or outside of React components.

subscribe.title: subscribe()
subscribe.description: Subscribe to state changes. Called whenever the state updates.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Find answers to the most common questions about Zustand state management.

what-is-zustand.a: Zustand is a small, fast, and scalable state management solution for React. It uses hooks and doesn't require providers or context, making it simpler to use than many alternatives.

why-use-zustand.q: Why should I use Zustand over other state management libraries?
why-use-zustand.a: Zustand offers a minimal API with no boilerplate, excellent TypeScript support, great performance out of the box, and a tiny bundle size (~1KB). It's perfect for projects of any size.

zustand-vs-redux.q: How does Zustand compare to Redux?
zustand-vs-redux.a: Zustand is much simpler than Redux with no reducers, action types, or dispatch. It has a smaller bundle size and requires less code. However, Redux has a larger ecosystem and may be better for very large teams.

bundle-size.a: Zustand is incredibly lightweight at around 1KB gzipped. This makes it one of the smallest state management libraries available.

create-store.q: How do I create a Zustand store?
create-store.a: Creating a store is simple using the create function. The store is just a hook that you can use directly in your components.

update-state.q: How do I update state in Zustand?
update-state.a: Use the set function provided to your store. You can pass a partial state object or an updater function.

async-actions.q: How do I handle async actions?
async-actions.a: Async actions work naturally in Zustand. Just make your action function async and call set when data is ready.

selectors.q: How do I use selectors to optimize re-renders?
selectors.a: Pass a selector function to your store hook to subscribe to specific parts of state. Use shallow comparison for object selections.

middleware.a: Zustand provides built-in middleware for persistence (persist), DevTools integration (devtools), Immer support (immer), and more. You can also create custom middleware.

persist-state.q: How do I persist state to localStorage?
persist-state.a: Use the persist middleware to automatically save and restore state from localStorage or sessionStorage.

devtools.a: Yes! Use the devtools middleware to enable Redux DevTools support for debugging your stores.

typescript.q: How well does Zustand work with TypeScript?
typescript.a: Zustand has excellent TypeScript support with full type inference. You can type your store state and actions for a great developer experience.

rerender-issues.q: Why is my component re-rendering too often?
rerender-issues.a: Make sure you're using selectors to subscribe to only the state you need. Use shallow comparison when selecting objects or arrays.

state-not-updating.a: Check that you're using the set function correctly. Remember that set merges state by default - use set(state, true) to replace the entire state.

ssr-issues.q: How do I handle SSR with Zustand?
ssr-issues.a: For SSR, make sure to handle hydration properly. Use the persist middleware's onRehydrateStorage callback or skip hydration on the server.

memory-leaks.q: How do I prevent memory leaks?
memory-leaks.a: Zustand handles cleanup automatically when components unmount. For manual subscriptions with subscribe(), make sure to call the returned unsubscribe function.

stillHaveQuestions.title: Still Have Questions?
stillHaveQuestions.description: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Join our community!

compare.title: State Management Comparison
compare.description: Compare Zustand with other popular React state management libraries to find the best fit for your project.

zustand.name: Zustand
zustand.summary: Simple, fast, and scalable. Perfect for most React projects with minimal boilerplate.

redux.name: Redux Toolkit
redux.summary: Industry standard with large ecosystem. Best for very large teams and complex apps.

mobx.name: MobX
mobx.summary: Reactive programming with automatic tracking. Great for complex derived state.

jotai.name: Jotai
jotai.summary: Primitive and flexible atomic state. Similar philosophy to Zustand from the same team.

recoil.name: Recoil
recoil.summary: Facebook's atomic state library. Good for complex async data dependencies.

zustandVsRedux.title: Zustand vs Redux
zustandVsRedux.zustand1: No boilerplate - create stores with minimal code
zustandVsRedux.zustand2: No providers needed - use stores anywhere
zustandVsRedux.redux2: Time-travel debugging out of the box
zustandVsRedux.redux3: Better for very large teams with strict patterns

zustandVsMobx.title: Zustand vs MobX
zustandVsMobx.zustand1: Simpler mental model - explicit state updates

useZustand.title: Use Zustand When
useZustand.reason1: You want minimal boilerplate and setup
useZustand.reason2: You're building a small to medium project
useZustand.reason3: You prefer a simple, hook-based API
useZustand.reason4: Bundle size is important to you

useOthers.title: Consider Alternatives When
useOthers.reason1: You need extensive Redux ecosystem tools
useOthers.reason2: Your team is already familiar with MobX
